# Galactic Interstellar Announces Successful Maiden Flight of Shenzhou-50, Mars Travel Prices Drop to 2 Million Yuan

**Date**: June 18, 2090  
**Author**: Space Journalist, Star Wanderer

---

## Key Announcement

Today, **Galactic Interstellar Travel Corporation**, the world's leading commercial space travel company, held a grand press conference at the **Wenchang Space Launch Center**, officially announcing the successful maiden flight of its new-generation manned spacecraft—**Shenzhou-50**!

**Li Xinghe**, Founder and CEO of Galactic Interstellar, excitedly stated at the conference: "The successful maiden flight of Shenzhou-50 marks a new era in human space travel. Our goal is to let everyone experience the charm of space!"

---

## Shenzhou-50 Highlights

### 1. Revolutionary Propulsion System

Shenzhou-50 is equipped with Galactic Interstellar's independently developed **"Galactic Engine" nuclear-powered propulsion system**, a major breakthrough in the history of human spaceflight:
- Travel time from Earth to Mars reduced from 6 months to **30 days**
- Propulsion efficiency increased by 500%
- Maximum speed reaches 2% of the speed of light

### 2. Comfortable Space Capsule Experience

Shenzhou-50 can accommodate 20 passengers, providing a luxurious and comfortable space travel experience:
- Each cabin is equipped with a panoramic sunroof, offering magnificent views of the universe
- Zero-gravity fitness center and leisure entertainment area
- Artificial gravity chamber to reduce space discomfort
- Michelin 3-star space restaurant

### 3. Safety and Security System

For the special risks of space travel, Shenzhou-50 provides multiple layers of safety and security:
- Triple redundant life support system
- Micro black hole shield
- Emergency return capsule, capable of returning to Earth within 24 hours
- Compliant with ISO Space Safety Certification

---

## Space Team

The R&D of Shenzhou-50 was led by **Wang Yuzhou**, CTO of Galactic Interstellar, with core team members including:
- **Zhang Xingchen**: Chief Designer, previously involved in the construction of the International Space Station
- **Liu Xingyun**: Propulsion System Lead, nuclear power expert
- **Zhao Yinhe**: Mission Planning Director, with 20 years of space mission experience
- **Sun Xingji**: Security System Architect

The entire R&D team consists of more than 5,000 people and took 10 years to complete the development of Shenzhou-50.

---

## Strategic Partnerships

At the conference, Galactic Interstellar also announced strategic partnerships with several organizations:
- **International Astronautical Agency (IAA)**: Co-develop deep space exploration technology
- **Mars Base Construction Company**: Build the first commercial base on Mars
- **Space Hotel Group**: Co-operate orbital space station hotels
- **Interstellar Travel Alliance**: Establish global space travel standards

---

## Development History

Galactic Interstellar was founded in 2050, headquartered in Shanghai:
- October 2050: Received angel round financing of ¥1 billion
- May 2055: Completed Series A financing of ¥5 billion
- August 2060: Successful maiden flight of Shenzhou-30, achieving suborbital travel
- March 2070: Series B financing of ¥20 billion, valuation reached ¥100 billion
- December 2080: Successful maiden flight of Shenzhou-40, achieving lunar commercial travel
- June 2090: Successful maiden flight of Shenzhou-50, ushering in the era of Mars travel

---

## Pricing and Booking

Shenzhou-50 Mars travel will officially accept bookings from October 1, 2090:
- **Economy Class**: ¥2 million per person
- **Business Class**: ¥5 million per person
- **Luxury Class**: ¥20 million per person

Early booking users can enjoy a 50% discount!

---

## Market Outlook

Industry analysts believe that the release of Shenzhou-50 will completely transform human travel. It is expected that by 2100, the global space travel market will exceed ¥10 trillion.

Galactic Interstellar stated that it will continue to invest in R&D, planning to launch **Shenzhou-60** in 2095 to achieve Saturn travel, and achieve commercial travel to any planet in the solar system by 2100.

---

"The universe is so big, we all want to see it!" —— Li Xinghe, Founder of Galactic Interstellar
